title: Sour Fluffy Filipino Brussels Sprouts Stir-fry
description: A low-carb-friendly date-night dish featuring tender Brussels sprouts cooked in a tangy sauce for a delightful and satisfying meal.

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce
ingredients.notes: Ensure the sauce is not too salty

ingredients.name: Water or Chicken Broth
ingredients.notes: To dilute tamarind paste and soy sauce if needed

instructions: Heat olive oil in a wok or large skillet over medium heat.
Add garlic, green onions, and red bell pepper. Stir-fry for about 2 minutes until fragrant and slightly softened.
Remove from heat and set aside to cool slightly.
In another pan, heat soy sauce and tamarind paste together with a little water or broth if needed to dissolve the tamarind paste (it should be smooth).
Return the cooled vegetables to the wok along with the sauce mixture. Stir-fry for about 5 minutes until the veggies are tender yet crunchy.
Stir in cornstarch mixed with water, and simmer until the sauce thickens slightly.
Serve hot as a side dish or over rice.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
